summary: Small rectangular single-story building with a raised brick roof inset and a surrounding low white stone curb. Walls are layered: a lower wooden/plank base, a sandstone midsection with horizontal windows, a white smooth band, and a brick top course beneath a stone slab coping. Stone-brick corner/column blocks accent the corners. The interior/roof appears recessed and covered by brick blocks with a stone slab border.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular box with an inner recessed roof area
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long axis (windows repeated evenly)

materials.name: sandstone
materials.role: main wall (below smooth band) / window surround

materials.name: quartz_smooth
materials.role: white horizontal band and outer curb

elements: continuous horizontal band of windows on long sides (3-4 panes each side)
continuous white smooth band between brick and sandstone
raised inner brick roof recessed from coping
outer low white curb/planter surrounding building on ground
rebuild_hints: Create a rectangular footprint roughly 11 x 19 blocks. Raise walls ~5 blocks tall then add a 1-block thick brick roof recessed by one block from stone slab coping.

Wall layering from bottom: 1-2 rows of wood planks, ~2 rows of sandstone with evenly spaced 1x2 horizontal glass windows, 1 row of smooth white blocks, 1 row of brick, then stone slab coping around roof edge.
Place stonebrick/chiseled-style vertical corner blocks 2-3 blocks tall at each corner interrupting the white band and sandstone layers.
Surround the base with a 1-block-high white curb offset by one block from the wall (visible as a border).
uncertainties: Exact block types for the white band and curb (appears to be smooth quartz but could be another white smooth block).
Lower base material: looks like wooden planks but could be a different brown wood variant.
Corner blocks: appear darker stone (stonebrick or polished andesite/diorite); identification not certain.
Internal height and any interior partitions are not visible.
Precise footprint dimensions: estimated from visible window spacing and top-down view; could be off by a few blocks.
